chunk_summary: This section discusses malnutrition severity classification and growth trajectory triggers in chronic cardiorespiratory disease, emphasizing bedside nutrition assessment and risk stratification using WHO growth standards and pediatric malnutrition guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the classification of malnutrition severity in pediatric patients with chronic cardiorespiratory disease.
Identify growth trajectory triggers using WHO child growth and weight velocity standards.
Apply bedside nutrition assessment techniques to stratify malnutrition risk.
Incorporate evidence-based guidelines for managing severe acute malnutrition.
key_facts: WHO child growth standards provide benchmarks for weight-for-age, height-for-age, and weight velocity (Reference 1, 2).
Severe acute malnutrition (SAM) is defined by weight-for-height Z-scores below -3 or the presence of bilateral pitting edema (Reference 4).
Growth trajectory monitoring is critical in chronic cardiorespiratory disease to prevent long-term complications.
Indicators for pediatric malnutrition include inadequate weight gain velocity and failure to thrive (Reference 3).
Bedside nutrition assessment tools should include anthropometric measurements and clinical history.
reference_quote: "Severe acute malnutrition is defined by weight-for-height Z-scores below -3 or the presence of bilateral pitting edema." (Reference 4)
